skill.name: when-releasing-new-product-orchestrate-product-launch
skill.description: Orchestrates an end-to-end product launch workflow from market research through post-launch monitoring. The skill defines a five-phase, ten-week process with specialist agents for planning, development, marketing, launch execution, and optimization.
skill.summary: Coordinate a complete product launch with phased planning, delivery, go-to-market execution, and post-launch optimization.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found most severe labels were false positives caused by product launch terminology and fenced command examples. The confirmed risks are operational: the skill instructs users to run external claude-flow commands and export launch data to /tmp. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malware behavior, unauthorized network endpoints, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External command orchestration examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes many fenced shell examples that run npx claude-flow commands, initialize swarms, spawn agents, store memory, execute workflows, and run helper scripts. This appears to be intended workflow automation rather than malicious execution, but users should review commands before running them because they can start external tooling and operate on project data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and numerous, so the execution surface is clear. Confidence is below very high because the commands are documentation examples for a launch workflow, not hidden or clearly malicious code.

low_findings.title: Temporary directory export may expose launch data
low_findings.description: The post-workflow summary example writes retrieved launch memory and workflow exports to /tmp paths. This can expose proprietary launch data on shared systems if users run the commands without adjusting output paths and permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The /tmp file writes are directly shown in the script example. The impact depends on runtime environment permissions and the sensitivity of launch memory data.

low_findings.title: Static keyword findings are false positives
low_findings.description: The critical and high static findings for Windows SAM, C2, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance are keyword collisions in launch planning prose, acronyms, file names, or diagram labels. No evidence found of SAM database access, command-and-control behavior, weak cryptographic implementation, network scanning, or system reconnaissance intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows benign product launch terminology such as TAM/SAM/SOM, SOC2, mobile apps, and workflow graph labels. Targeted review found no matching malicious capability behind those keywords.

dangerous_patterns.title: External command orchestration examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes many fenced shell examples that run npx claude-flow commands, initialize swarms, spawn agents, store memory, execute workflows, and run helper scripts. This appears to be intended workflow automation rather than malicious execution, but users should review commands before running them because they can start external tooling and operate on project data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and numerous, so the execution surface is clear. Confidence is below very high because the commands are documentation examples for a launch workflow, not hidden or clearly malicious code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Temporary directory export may expose launch data
dangerous_patterns.description: The post-workflow summary example writes retrieved launch memory and workflow exports to /tmp paths. This can expose proprietary launch data on shared systems if users run the commands without adjusting output paths and permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The /tmp file writes are directly shown in the script example. The impact depends on runtime environment permissions and the sensitivity of launch memory data.

content.value_statement: Product launches fail when planning, engineering, marketing, sales, and support work from disconnected plans. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a phased workflow for coordinating launch work from research through optimization.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase launch process covering research, development, marketing, launch execution, and post-launch optimization.
Maps responsibilities across specialist agents for product, engineering, security, QA, marketing, sales, support, and operations.
Provides claude-flow command examples for swarm initialization, agent spawning, memory storage, workflow execution, and reporting.
Specifies memory namespace conventions so phase deliverables can be stored and retrieved across the launch timeline.
Includes success criteria and metrics for technical readiness, business outcomes, marketing performance, and support quality.
Includes a GraphViz workflow diagram that shows phases, dependencies, gates, memory coordination, and monitoring loops.
limitations: It depends on claude-flow commands and compatible agent types being available in the user environment.
It provides workflow guidance and examples, not a guaranteed launch outcome or automated project management system.
The ten-week timeline is a template and may need adjustment for regulated, enterprise, or hardware launches.
Users must review shell commands, memory contents, and export paths before running examples with sensitive data.

use_cases.title: Plan a SaaS Launch
use_cases.description: Create a coordinated launch plan that connects market research, product strategy, development, marketing, sales enablement, and weekly optimization.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Cross-Functional Release Work
use_cases.description: Assign clear phase outputs and dependencies across engineering, QA, security, devops, marketing, support, and sales teams.

use_cases.title: Prepare Launch Readiness Gates
use_cases.description: Build go/no-go checks for production validation, security review, campaign readiness, support operations, and post-launch monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Launch Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: define the baseline launch workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a ten-week product launch plan for [product]. Include phases, owners, deliverables, success criteria, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: tailor phases to a specific product and team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt the product launch workflow for [product type] with a [team size] team and a launch date of [date]. Show what should run sequentially and what can run in parallel.

prompt_templates.title: Build Readiness Checks
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: prepare launch gates and accountability.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow to define launch readiness gates for engineering, security, marketing, sales, support, and operations. Include go/no-go criteria and required evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Orchestrate Post-Launch Optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: run monitoring and improvement loops after launch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a post-launch optimization cadence for [product]. Include weekly metrics, specialist reviews, experiment ideas, memory outputs, and escalation triggers.

output_examples.input: Launch a B2B analytics product in ten weeks with a small engineering team.
output_examples.output: A phased launch plan with market validation, MVP delivery, sales enablement, production readiness checks, launch week activities, and weekly optimization reviews.

output_examples.input: Prepare go/no-go criteria before public launch.
output_examples.output: A readiness checklist covering test status, security review, performance targets, rollback planning, monitoring, support staffing, campaign readiness, and stakeholder approval.

output_examples.input: Coordinate marketing and product work during development.
output_examples.output: A parallel execution plan that lets marketing, content, SEO, and sales enablement start from product strategy while engineering completes implementation.

best_practices: Confirm agent names, tooling, and memory paths before running any command examples.
Review each phase deliverable with accountable stakeholders before moving to the next launch gate.
Store only approved launch artifacts in shared memory and protect sensitive product, customer, and revenue data.
anti_patterns: Do not run the shell examples unchanged in production environments without reviewing paths, permissions, and installed tools.
Do not treat the default ten-week timeline as fixed when compliance, procurement, or app store review adds constraints.
Do not skip post-launch monitoring after release, because the workflow depends on feedback loops and metric reviews.

faq.question: What kind of product launch is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is best for software, SaaS, mobile, and major feature launches that need coordinated technical and go-to-market work.

faq.question: Does it execute the launch automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow steps and command examples. Users must decide what to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Which tools does it assume?
faq.answer: The workflow references claude-flow commands, agent spawning, memory storage, hooks, and GraphViz documentation.

faq.question: Can it support smaller launches?
faq.answer: Yes. Users can reduce agent count, shorten phases, and combine roles for smaller teams or simpler releases.

faq.question: What should users check before running commands?
faq.answer: They should verify installed tools, command arguments, memory contents, file export paths, and access to sensitive launch data.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes external command examples and temporary file exports. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection.
